team_spec.vision: A small CLI that pairs a season's kelp-bed condition assessments with the stewardship actions that happened in each bioregion, so a community group can read which beds are stressed AND what work has been showing up around them.
team_spec.spec: Single-file Python CLI that reads two JSON files (observations + actions), groups by bioregion, and prints a joint report: per-site kelp condition + per-action-type counts, with a summary line. Composed from Kelp Bed Observers' condition-assessment tool and Steward Calendar's bioregion/month grouping; the calendar shape is collapsed to seasonal totals in this v0 composition.
team_spec.build_target: one single-file Python tool, standard library only

freeze_record.frozen_by: Kelp Bed Observers + Steward Calendar (both teams, joint re-freeze) + facilitator
freeze_record.joint_freeze_note: Both teams re-read the candidate bundle (intersected authorization = display_scope: partial, ai_input_scope: partial, reuse_scope: ask-first), confirmed the cleared inputs from each team, and re-confirmed that neither team's protected boundary is dissolved by composition. Re-freeze conversation transcript lives in the joint witness record (untracked-allocation section).

allowed_inputs.name: sample-observations.json
allowed_inputs.note: sample of the cleared kelp canopy observations dataset (public-shoreline only, observer aliases only)

allowed_inputs.name: sample-actions.json
allowed_inputs.note: sample of the cleared public-events stewardship action log (site aliases only)

excluded_inputs.boundary: Observer real names and contact details — held in the team's roster spreadsheet; only aliases appear in any input or output.
excluded_inputs.note: Content is deliberately not in this packet; the build attempt never receives it.

excluded_inputs.boundary: Host-Nation stewardship cycle reference — a partner-organization reference document held by the team. Shapes private team thinking, but is never in any AI input or display.
excluded_inputs.note: Content is deliberately not in this packet. Composition does not dissolve this boundary; it is carried forward unchanged.

excluded_inputs.boundary: Volunteer real names — held in team's roster spreadsheet; only site_alias appears in any input.
excluded_inputs.note: Content is deliberately not in this packet.

display_allowed_parts: the joint kelp-condition + stewardship-action report computed from the two cleared sample fixtures only
ai_allowed_parts: the cleared kelp canopy observations (public-shoreline, aliases only)
the cleared public-events stewardship action log (site aliases only)
intersected_authorization.authorization_notes: Composed authorization — most-restrictive-wins across both source submissions. Both teams re-confirmed. Reuse forced to ask-first by composition.

description: AC1 - prints header 'KELP-BED STEWARDSHIP REPORT (<B> bioregions)' where B is distinct bioregions across both inputs
AC2 - one section per bioregion (alphabetical asc) with a KELP: block and an ACTIONS: block
AC3 - KELP block: per-site mean canopy (rounded half-away-from-zero) -> condition (healthy >=60, declining 30-59, stressed <30); sites alphabetical asc; 'none' line when bioregion has no observations
AC4 - ACTIONS block: per-action-type counts, count desc then alpha asc; 'none' line when bioregion has no actions; unknown action_type values dropped
AC5 - blank line then SUMMARY line with total healthy/declining/stressed sites and total valid actions
AC6 - handles empty inputs and malformed JSON (single 'error:' line, exit 0)
AC7 - missing CLI argument prints 'error:' and exits 0
AC8 - stdlib only; no network, no credential, no DB; output never references excluded/marker-only content

review_checks: the generated tool runs and exits cleanly with no network, credential, or database access
the tool passes every acceptance-criteria test
no excluded / marker-only record (host-Nation stewardship cycle reference, real observer/volunteer names) appears in the tool or its output
no outputs are hardcoded for specific inputs - the tool is correct in general
the joint witness record states only what happened and does not certify, approve, or grant reuse
both teams' authorizations are honored: only the cleared public fixtures are in scope for the build attempt

fields: what did not work / what broke
what we learned (per team's perspective + joint)
witness_record_seed.receipt_statement: This record states what happened. It does not establish authority, approval, certification, legitimacy, community consent, or readiness for reuse. Composition does not dissolve either team's boundaries; both teams' marker-only records remain marker-only after this build attempt and after this witness record.

boundary: This packet is a CANDIDATE-then-RE-FROZEN composition of two team submissions. It carries both teams' boundaries forward unchanged; it does not establish authority, approval, certification, or reuse permission for either source team's offerings or for the composed tool.
